title: Nanoblade production
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eomka","text":"Nanoblades are virus-like particles that are able to transiently deliver a Cas9 protein together with a gRNA, without integration of DNA in the genome.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bajdk","text":"Production of Virus-like particles is always performed in a Bio Safety Lab 2 (BSL-2 lab)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6u04r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9adec","text":"BSL-2: Here, it is permitted to work with organisms that cause diseases. However, the lab is only accessible to people who work there and know the procedures. Doors are always closed during work and windows cannot be opened. The entire area is set up for efficient cleaning and disinfection. All waste is disinfected.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"58cui","text":"Desinfect the flow with 70 % Ethanol before starting any work there. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c8aeq","text":"Always wear to sets of gloves and a sterile lab coat.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5vvik","text":"Desinfect bottles and tip boxes with 70 % Ethanol, before entering the flow and after the work is finished.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fn1fc","text":"Desinfect gloves regularly with 70 % Ethanol.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9q020","text":"Waste is collected in a beaker filled with bleach.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"c7kv4","text":"Be careful not to mix bleach with alcohol-derived reagents. The key ingredient in household bleach is sodium hypochlorite. Sodium hypochlorite reacts with ethanol, isopropyl alcohol, and other types of alcohol to make chloroform (CHCl3), hydrochloric acid (HCl), and other compounds, such as dichloroacetate or chloroacetone.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":193,"length":17},{"style":"sub","offset":234,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6maee","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: SEEDING Petri-dishes (PD)
Seed HEK293T cells in a 10 cm PD in DMEM 2% FCS + 500 µL Gentamycin
Incubate on 37 °C at 5% CO 2
TRANSFECTION of transfer, packaging, and envelop plasmids
Before starting, measure the DNA concentration and the purity of the plasmids in the molecular lab.

Make a DNA mixture in an 1,5 mL tube of the following plasmids: A B C 1 1 PD 5 PD 2 GAG-POL (MLV-gag-pol Els Verhoeyen**) 6,38 µG 31,9 µG 3 BIC-Cas9 (BIC-SpCas9 Els Verhoeyen**) 6,38 µG 31,9 µG 4 Crizi-sgRNA* (Crizi Els Verhoeyen**) 6,38 µG 31,9 µG 5 VSV-G (VSVG Els Verhoeyen**) 1,59 µG 7,95 µG 6 BaevR (hCMV-BaevR Less ClaI-HpaI**) 1,59 µG 7,95 µG *This plasmid changes, depending on the gRNA **plasmid name from the database
Write down the total volume of the DNA mixture
Transfer the DNA mixture in a 50 mL Falcon tube in the vector lab, under the flow
Add autoclaved PBS to the mixture with a final volume of 557 µL (1 PD) or 2785 µL (5 PD)
Dilute the linear PEI (323 mg/L) with autoclaved PBS: A B C 1 1 PD 5 PD 2 Linear PEI 245,5 µL 1227,5 µL 3 PBS 311,0 µL 1555,0 µL
Add the diluted linear PEI to the DNA mixtures GENTLY , and incubate for 5 to 10 minutes on RT
Add 5 mL (1 PD) or 25 mL (5 PD) of cold medium (DMEM 2% FCS + 500 µL Gentamycin or OptiMem + 500 µL Gentamycin) to the DNA-PEI mixtures GENTLY and mix
Check the seeded HEK293T cells under the microscope for confluency
Remove the medium on the HEK293T cells and add the transfection medium. Normally there is enough for 6 mL of medium per PD
Incubate on 37 °C at 5% CO 2
REMOVING TRANSFECTION MEDIUM
Remove the Transfection medium and replace it with RT medium. 5 mL per PD should be sufficient
Leave the medium flask at RT
Incubate on 37 °C at 5% CO 2
1 st HARVEST
Collect the supernatant from the PD using a 0,45 µM syringe and store at 4 °C
Also store the medium flask at 37 °C
Add Pre-warmed medium to the cells.
Incubate on 37 °C at 5% CO 2
2 nd HARVEST
Collect the supernatant from the PD using a 0,45 µM syringe and store at 4 °C
Desinfect the PD by spraying 70% Ethanol or bleach on the cells, before throwing them in the yellow bin

The supernatant of both harvest days will be concentrated via vivaspin 1. First wash the vivaspin membrane by adding 10 mL of PBS and spinning down at 3000g for 3 minutes 2. Add the supernatant to the vivaspin membrane and spin down at 3000g, until there is about 0,5 - 1,0 mL remaining
Nanoblades can be stored at -80 °C
creator: maxime smits
